name: Lefkada
subtitle: The island you drive to — white cliffs, turquoise lagoons and the Ionian's finest beaches
intro: Lefkada is unique among the Ionian islands — connected to the mainland by a floating bridge, so no ferry required. Yet despite the easy access it feels genuinely remote, especially in the south where sheer white limestone cliffs plunge into waters of an impossible blue-green. Porto Katsiki and Egremni are consistently ranked among the best beaches in Europe. The island is also the windsurfing capital of Greece at Vassiliki.

itinerary.title: 4-Day Lefkada Itinerary
itinerary.subtitle: From the salt lagoon causeway to the white cliffs of the south — Nydri, Vassiliki, Porto Katsiki and the hidden beaches of the west coast.

stops.name: Lefkada Town
stops.desc: Charming Ionian town with distinctive earthquake-resistant architecture — upper floors clad in corrugated metal and painted wood. Walk the pedestrian main street and the seafront.

stops.name: Santa Maura Fortress
stops.desc: 14th-century Venetian fortress at the causeway entrance. The saltwater lagoon and the floating bridge make for an extraordinary approach to the island.

stops.name: Gyra Lagoon
stops.desc: The salt lagoon on the west side of the causeway — a protected wetland with flamingoes, herons and windmills. The beach on the outer side has calm shallow water.

stops.name: Agios Nikitas
stops.desc: The most picturesque village on the west coast — a tiny car-free harbour with excellent fish tavernas. Lunch here then walk to the hidden Milos beach below.

stops.name: Kathisma Beach
stops.desc: Long sandy beach on the west coast with dramatic cliffs at the southern end. One of the finest beaches in the northern half of the island.

stops.name: Nydri
stops.desc: Main resort town on the east coast with views over the Ionian islands of Meganisi, Skorpios and Sparti. Boat trips depart from here to the surrounding islands.

stops.name: Skorpios Island Cruise
stops.desc: Day cruise around Onassis's private island Skorpios — you can swim in the crystal coves around it. Boats depart from Nydri harbour every morning.

stops.name: Meganisi Island
stops.desc: Short ferry from Nydri to the beautiful neighbouring island. The harbour village of Spartochori is one of the most authentic in the Ionian. Excellent lunch at a waterfront taverna.

stops.name: Vassiliki
stops.desc: World-famous windsurfing village at the southern tip. The afternoon thermal winds are legendary among windsurfers. Even non-surfers enjoy the dramatic setting and excellent seafood tavernas.

stops.name: Egremni Beach
stops.desc: One of the longest and most dramatic beaches in Greece — 1.5km of white pebble beneath sheer white cliffs, accessible only by 350 steps or by boat from Vassiliki. Completely wild and unorganised.

stops.name: Porto Katsiki
stops.desc: Consistently ranked one of the best beaches in Europe — sheer white limestone cliffs 100m high dropping to turquoise water. Access via 100 steps. Arrive by 11am for a sunbed. Unmissable.

stops.name: Athani Village
stops.desc: Tiny clifftop village above Porto Katsiki with spectacular views. The local taverna serves the best food in southern Lefkada — lamb from the hillside farms, local wine.

stops.name: Cape Lefkatas
stops.desc: The southernmost tip of Lefkada — a sheer white cape where, according to legend, Sappho threw herself into the sea for unrequited love. Lighthouse and extraordinary sunset views.

stops.name: Poros Beach
stops.desc: Remote west-coast beach accessible only by a narrow road. Long, pebbly, wild and almost always empty. One of the hidden beaches of the island.

stops.name: Gialos Beach
stops.desc: Quiet pebble beach on the west coast — calm, clear water and a small taverna. Much less visited than the famous southern beaches.

stops.name: Agios Petros Village
stops.desc: Traditional inland village with a good local taverna. Try the local cheeses and the Lefkada sausage (loukanika) before the drive north.

stops.name: Lefkada Town — Departure
stops.desc: Return across the floating bridge to the mainland. No ferry needed — Lefkada is the only Greek island you can drive to and from.

beaches.name: Porto Katsiki
beaches.depth: Medium to deep — dramatic drop-off, exceptional clarity
beaches.facing: West-facing — sheltered by the cliffs, calm water most days
beaches.facilities: Basic: one beach bar, sunbeds (pricey). 100 steps down from the car park. Boat access from Vassiliki.
beaches.desc: Consistently ranked one of the best beaches in Europe — sheer white limestone cliffs 100m high drop into water of an extraordinary turquoise-blue. The setting is almost theatrical. One of those beaches where the first view from the top of the steps genuinely takes your breath away.

beaches.name: Egremni Beach
beaches.depth: Medium to deep — crystal clear Ionian water
beaches.facing: West-facing — open sea, best in calm weather
beaches.facilities: None — completely wild. 350 steps from the road or boat from Vassiliki (20 min).
beaches.desc: The wilder sibling of Porto Katsiki — a vast sweep of white pebble beneath sheer cliffs, 1.5km long and almost entirely empty because of the 350-step descent. The effort is the price of solitude. The water is some of the clearest in Greece.

beaches.name: Kathisma Beach
beaches.depth: Shallow to medium — good for all swimmers
beaches.facilities: Good: sunbeds, beach bars, taverna, parking. Dramatic cliffs at the southern end.
beaches.desc: The best easily accessible beach on the west coast — a long sandy stretch with dramatic cliff scenery at one end and a relaxed beach bar atmosphere. Much less crowded than Porto Katsiki and far more family-friendly.

beaches.name: Milos Beach (Agios Nikitas)
beaches.facilities: Basic: one taverna on the beach. Access on foot from Agios Nikitas (15 min walk) or by water taxi.
beaches.desc: A beautiful hidden pebble cove below the village of Agios Nikitas, inaccessible by car. The 15-minute walk keeps it quiet. Crystal clear water, a small taverna, and the prettiest village on the island directly above.

beaches.name: Pefkoulia Beach
beaches.facilities: Basic: small beach bar, limited sunbeds. Quiet and uncrowded.
beaches.desc: A long, relatively uncrowded beach just south of Agios Nikitas. Cedar trees provide natural shade at the back of the beach. The water is calm and clear. One of the best spots on the island for watching the sun set over the Ionian.
